session_note: S76 batch (user-approved autonomous, user away). P1 hunter 2.5x faster; P2 rainbow more common; P3 complexity-income scoring (Full) — replaces the monotonic placement-accumulator with a per-tick income rate proportional to standing structure complexity, so destruction slows point-gain, and unifies the scoring path so player-1 scores identically to everyone. Council R1 (Claude+Grok-4.20-reasoning+Gemini-2.5-pro) + PRIME-AUDIT done.
council_battle_ledger: S76 Council R1 (Claude+Grok-4.20-reasoning DISRUPTOR+Gemini-2.5-pro AUDITOR) on the P3 scoring redesign (income vs snapshot). CONVERGED: income model (per-tick rate proportional to standing complexity) faithfully serves the user's literal 'gain points slower / complexity makes you gain faster' (a RATE, not a clawback); Gemini judged it architecturally sound (score follows persistent state, destruction becomes meaningful). DELTAS: D1 CRITICAL (Grok) complexity formula corrected N_prims+2*N_magic (R1 draft 'bond-weights+isolated-count' PUNISHED functional bonding 2->1 = a don't-connect exploit) -> functional-neutral + matches old accumulator for trees so threshold 50 stays meaningful. D2 CRITICAL (Gemini) single-pass GLOBAL ownership attribution (each bond/prim to one placedBy) -> no double-count if a cross-color bond ever exists. D3 HIGH (Gemini) Math.floor(scoreProgress)>=threshold for WIN + hunter-trigger (float-safe; HUD 50/50 coincides with win). D4 MED (Gemini) tickScoring host-only; client reads snapshot scoreProgress (host-authoritative preserved). D5 (Grok mitigation) keep SCORE_TIER pulses + BOND_COMMIT/structure-grow visuals so placement still has feedback despite no score-number jump. REJECTED: Grok's 'monotonic + 30-40% destruction tax' alternative -- a clawback, not the user's stated 'gain slower' rate model (logged as a future option). P1/P2 are Micro tunables (Council auto-waived on the user-approval path; covered by the batch).
prime_audit: S76 PRIME-AUDIT (Full). The Council MATERIALLY improved the R1 design (D1 caught a perverse-incentive bug in the complexity metric; D2 hardened ownership; D3 the float-win precision class). No rubber-stamping: every delta is an evidence-backed change, not a claim-addressed-not-fixed. No consensus-masking: Grok pushed feel/runaway concerns, Gemini pushed correctness; both surfaced distinct, adopted findings. Runtime-Verifiability (mandatory, runtime infra): the income loop runs in the live 60Hz host tick path -> the Playwright gating lane is a real-browser boot-then-smoke (score accrues + WIN fires + hunter triggers post-build), NOT a static parse. Determinism: tickScoring is a pure fn of (state,tick), host-only, result serialized to clients -> replay-safe, no cross-machine float divergence (clients never compute). Mtime N/A (new code). Biggest residual = BALANCE (un-playtestable while user away) -> mitigated by making SCORE_INCOME_PER_COMPLEXITY_PER_SEC the #1 flagged one-line tunable. Confidence HIGH on correctness/architecture; balance is a dial for the user's next playtest.
council_waiver_reason: Micro-tier test-debt closure (1 NEW e2e spec file, ZERO production code; the seam exists). Micro deliberation auto-waived on the user-approval path. CHECK = RALPH:PATROL + GROK-ANALYST (Micro), not full Triumvirate.

priorities.label: S76 P1 (Micro) -- HUNTER 2.5x FASTER. constants.ts: HUNTER_MAX_SPEED 1.4->3.5, HUNTER_ACCEL 0.12->0.30 (both x2.5, preserves accel/maxspeed juke ratio; terminal 3.0 < cap 3.5), DAMPING 0.9 unchanged; update comment S75->S76. Verify hunter unit tests (literal args, no flip) + e2e/hunter.spec.ts (faster=catches sooner). NO protocol bump.
priorities.check_method: S76 P1 (hunter 2.5x faster; Micro) SHIPPED cbdf67c. constants.ts: HUNTER_MAX_SPEED 1.4->3.5 + HUNTER_ACCEL 0.12->0.30 (both x2.5; terminal accel/(1-damping)=3.0 px/tick sits under the 3.5 cap, same headroom shape as S75's 1.2<1.4 / pre-S75 6<7; accel/maxspeed juke ratio + DAMPING 0.9 unchanged). NO protocol bump (host-only constants). tsc -b --noEmit EXIT 0. Hunter UNIT tests pass literal pursuit args (0.6/7/0.9 in hunterLifecycle.test.ts) so the constant change cannot flip them; lifecycle assertions (gets-closer / catches-on-contact / escapes-when-unreachable) hold regardless of speed. e2e/hunter.spec.ts: a FASTER hunter catches SOONER than the S75-slow version, so the S75-widened catch-wait (90s) + test.setTimeout(120s) hold with margin. Full Playwright gating lane (hunter+rainbow+bomb+potato specs) deferred to P3 batch-end -- most valuable run once over the whole diff (S75 pattern). Token 268987/1M (26.9% GREEN).

priorities.label: S76 P2 (Micro) -- RAINBOW MORE COMMON. constants.ts: RAINBOW_SPAWN_MIN_SPARKS 35->15, RAINBOW_SPAWN_MAX_SPARKS 60->28 (still rarest hazard, now appears ~1.5-3min in). TTL unchanged. Update comment. NO protocol bump.
priorities.check_method: S76 P2 (rainbow more common; Micro) SHIPPED 7d7a2bd. constants.ts RAINBOW_SPAWN_MIN_SPARKS 35->15, RAINBOW_SPAWN_MAX_SPARKS 60->28 (still the rarest hazard -- potato 10-18, bomb 8-15 -- but ~1.5-3min to first rainbow vs the old ~4-7min). TTL/radius/max-active unchanged. NO protocol bump (host-only spawner cadence). Pure-literal change on the tsc-EXIT-0 P1 baseline. rainbow.spec.ts is INSULATED: the __TEST_RAINBOW_SPAWN_SPARKS__ seam forces both bounds to a fixed small test value, so the production 15-28 retune does not touch the e2e. Full Playwright gating lane at P3 batch-end. Token ~276000/1M (~27.6% GREEN; true-up at P3).

priorities.label: S76 P3 (Full) -- COMPLEXITY-INCOME SCORING. New scoring.ts (computeComplexity = N_prims + 2*N_magicBonds via single-pass global ownership; tickScoring host-only per-tick accrual scoreByPlayer[p]+=RATE*complexity/PHYSICS_HZ, scoreProgress=max for all modes). constants SCORE_INCOME_PER_COMPLEXITY_PER_SEC=0.15. Unify addScore single-path (fixes P1 inconsistency). Wire tickScoring host-only in main.ts before tickGameState. Remove placement addScore + sync SCORE_TIER from placePrimitive(+placeFromFree). Math.floor win+hunter gate (gameState.ts + main.ts). ui.ts Math.floor display. Rewrite session9/10/13/15 + gameState scoring tests; new scoring.test.ts (complexity/accrual/destruction-degrades/player-consistency/determinism). NO protocol bump. CHECK=Triumvirate + gating lane.
priorities.check_method: S76 P3 (complexity-income scoring; FULL) VERIFIED + SHIPPED 16023ae (pushed; CI E2E+Deploy in-flight -> confirm green per Rule 22). Replaced the monotonic per-PLACEMENT accumulator with a per-tick complexity-INCOME model: each host tick scoreByPlayer[p] += SCORE_INCOME_PER_COMPLEXITY_PER_SEC(0.15) * complexity(p) / PHYSICS_HZ; complexity = #prims + 2*#magicBonds (PRIM_WEIGHT=SCORE_ANCHOR, MAGIC_BONUS=SCORE_MAGIC_BOND-SCORE_FUNCTIONAL_BOND); scoreProgress = max(scoreByPlayer) UNIFIED for all modes -> fixes the #3b player-1 inconsistency by collapsing addScore's solo-additive-vs-networked-max dual path. NEW src/state/scoring.ts (computeComplexity single-pass GLOBAL-ownership + tickScoring host-only); main.ts calls tickScoring before tickGameState (host-only, PLAYING) + before the hunter trigger; Math.floor float-safe gate on WIN (gameState.ts) + hunter 75% (main.ts); placePrimitive scoring removed (3 addScore + SCORE_TIER emit -> tickScoring at the leader avatar); ui.ts floors the float display; gameMode.addScore unified. NO PROTOCOL bump (wire shape unchanged; stays v6). CHECK = TRIUMVIRATE: RALPH:PATROL CLEAN (all gates green) + GROK-ANALYST (grok-4.20-reasoning) SHIP + GEMINI-AUDITOR (gemini-2.5-pro) FIX-REFUTED. Gemini flagged 'a bond between two players prims credits only one owner non-deterministically' -> REFUTED with evidence (cross-colour bonds CANNOT form: placePrimitive demotes a cross-colour target to anchor + same-colour-filters merge candidates; placedBy is readonly; the rainbow remaps placerColor uniformly, never placedBy -> a bond's endpoints ALWAYS share one owner; aId is deterministic = the new prim in makeBond, host-authoritative -> replay-stable). Refutation + a future-Steal forward-note landed in scoring.ts spec body (never-drop-a-finding rule). Grok independently SHIP. PRIME-AUDIT runtime-verifiability: the income loop runs in the live 60Hz host tick and is PROVEN by the Playwright gating lane (the hunter's 75% trigger fired off REAL in-browser income; the 2-peer WIN pipeline propagated host->joiner). Determinism: pure fn of (state, tick), host-only, result serialized to clients (who never compute). GATES: tsc -b --noEmit 0; vitest 1142 pass (+12 net: NEW scoring.test.ts 13 [complexity/accrual/destruction-degrades/player-consistency/determinism/unified-addScore] + rewrote session9/10/13/14/15 + gameState scoring to the income model); vite build 534.35KB main (<550 charter; +0.42KB vs S75); Playwright gating lane 32 pass + 1 pre-existing Sym-E skip (incl income-driven hunter trigger + WIN pipeline; smoke Sym I + S62 FFA inject host score via __SPARK__.world to test the WIN PIPELINE robustly since income decouples placement from instant-win). #1 PLAYTEST TUNABLE: SCORE_INCOME_PER_COMPLEXITY_PER_SEC = 0.15 (range 0.05-0.25). Token 517952/1M (51.8% YELLOW).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #hunter-tuning #2.5x-faster #micro #playtest-two-way-dial: shipped cbdf67c. HUNTER_MAX_SPEED 1.4->3.5 + ACCEL 0.12->0.30 (both x2.5), reversing ~half of S75's 5x slowdown after live feedback that 1.4 px/tick posed no threat. DEFINING MOVE: scaled BOTH momentum constants by the SAME factor so the terminal-speed/cap headroom (3.0<3.5) and the accel/maxspeed ratio -- the momentum/overshoot juke character -- stay byte-identical to S75's shape; only absolute speed rises. Same 'scale the whole momentum model together, never one constant alone' lesson from S75 P2, applied in reverse. Unit tests insulated (literal pursuit args); a faster hunter only catches sooner so the wall-time-bounded e2e stays green without widening. LESSON: playtest tuning is a two-way dial -- S75's 5x cut over-corrected to 'no threat'; 2.5x back-up lands between pre-S75 'instant-eat' (7) and S75 'toothless' (1.4). The constant carries its own full version history in the comment so the next tuner sees the whole arc.

P2 #rainbow-rarity #micro #spawn-math #playtest: shipped 7d7a2bd. RAINBOW_SPAWN_MIN/MAX_SPARKS 35/60->15/28 after the rainbow never appeared in a live match. DEFINING MOVE: reasoned from the spawn MATH, not vibes -- at SPAWN_RATE_PER_SECOND=0.15 (1 spark / 6.7s), 35-60 sparks = 235-400s, i.e. the FIRST rainbow only ~4-7 min in, longer than a 2-player match runs; 15-28 sparks = ~100-187s (~1.5-3 min) so a match reliably sees one while it stays strictly rarer than the potato band (10-18). Verified the __TEST_RAINBOW_SPAWN_SPARKS__ seam still forces both bounds to a fixed test value, so rainbow.spec.ts is insulated from the production retune. LESSON: a 'spawn rate feels wrong' tune is a unit-conversion problem (sparks -> seconds via the base spawn rate) -- compute the expected first-appearance time, never eyeball the raw constant.

P3 #scoring-redesign #complexity-income #player-consistency #full #council-caught-formula-bug #gemini-refuted: shipped 16023ae. Replaced SPARK's monotonic per-placement accumulator with a per-tick INCOME whose rate is proportional to standing structure complexity (#prims + 2*#magicBonds), from live 2-player feedback that (a) destroying a structure had ZERO scoring consequence and (b) player-1 scored differently. DEFINING MOVES: (1) the PLAN-phase Council caught a CRITICAL bug in my R1 complexity formula -- 'sum of bond-weights + isolated-anchor count' PUNISHED functional bonding (two isolated prims = complexity 2; bond them functionally -> complexity 1), a 'don't-connect' exploit. Corrected to '#prims + 2*#magic', which is functional-NEUTRAL AND reproduces the old accumulator's value for a finished tree, so the 50-pt WIN gate needed ZERO recalibration. (2) fixed the player-1 bug STRUCTURALLY -- collapsed addScore's solo-additive-vs-networked-max branches into ONE path (scoreProgress = max(scoreByPlayer) always; solo = single-value max) so every player scores identically BY CONSTRUCTION, not by patching a symptom. (3) income reads LIVE standing complexity each tick, so destruction (bomb/potato/sever/disconnect) needs NO explicit hook -- 'lose structure -> gain slower' falls out for free. (4) CHECK Triumvirate: Gemini-AUDITOR raised a 'cross-player-bond non-determinism' FIX -- REFUTED with evidence (cross-colour bonds can't form; placedBy readonly; aId host-deterministic) and logged the refutation + a future-Steal forward-note in the spec body, rather than reflexively 'fixing' an impossible case. (5) e2e: the WIN-pipeline smoke tests won instantly on the 3rd anchor under the accumulator; under income that needs ~6.7s accrual AND risks a premature mid-build win that throws the next dragSparkTo, so I inject the host score via __SPARK__.world AFTER the build (the build->income->WIN path is covered by units + the solo hunter e2e, whose 75% trigger fires off REAL in-browser income). LESSON: when a model change decouples cause (placement) from effect (score), prefer STRUCTURAL fixes (unify the path) + LIVE-STATE derivation (no destruction hooks); and a CHECK finding premised on a violated invariant should be refuted-with-evidence + logged, not rubber-stamped into a needless fix. Balance is the #1 flagged playtest tunable (rate 0.15). Gates: tsc0 / vitest1142 / build534.35KB / gating 32+1skip.

fork_decisions.fork_option1_vs_2: Option 1 (stable lobby preview + DENSE Begin roster); Option 2 sparse-in-game deferred to #4
fork_decisions.fork_hole_fill: lowest-free seat for new joiners (incumbents never move; rack stays compact)
fork_decisions.fork_protocol_bump: NO bump (stays v5) -- wire shape unchanged; holed LOBBY_PRESENCE is a valid <=MAX array; client renders received roster

carry_forward: === RESOLVED S72 === P2 PAC-MAN HUNTER (97e4616): leader-reaches-75% spawns ONCE a host-authoritative hunter chasing the avatar via deterministic capped-momentum pursuit (juke-able); catch -> 30s bench (avatar hidden + input locked) + DROP_SPARK; 30s escape -> despawn. SEPARATE world.hunters Map; CRITICAL target-disconnect crash-guard; solo avatarPos fix; no protocol bump; additive snapshot. P3 POTATO BOMB (76af09a): carryable from-SPAWN-fuse (Fork E user) radial-AoE weapon; SEPARATE world.potatoes Map + separate potatoRng (spark+bomb byte-identical); carry-1 exclusive w/ spark (both directions); deterministic position-based AoE (squared-dist + sorted PrimitiveId/BondId; owner-agnostic; no chain reaction); carrier-disconnect force-detonate (sim-loop poll); NO protocol bump (Council batch-bump). P4 (309024c): detonation BOOM SFX (covers bomb+potato) + defensive applyStartGame bomb-clear. Teardown invariant for all 3 hazards on WIN/RETURN_TO_TITLE/START_GAME. === LOGGED CHECK CARRY-FORWARDS (S72, not dropped) === (a) NO-BUMP cosmetic mixed-version gap: a stale-v5 (P2-build) client joining a P3-build host sees prims vanish from a potato AoE without rendering the potato -- accepted friends-only trade-off (both CHECK reviewers validated no-bump); if a future feature needs cross-version safety, revisit the S52 new-client-intent->bump precedent. (b) Centralized PLAYING-exit teardown (Gemini P3): the current per-trigger (WIN_TRIGGER) + RETURN_TO_TITLE + START_GAME-invariant fully covers the actual FSM (no DRAW state), but a single PLAYING-exit teardown hook would be cleaner if the FSM grows. (c) spawner rng/bombRng/potatoRng not serialized -> save/load resets spawn cadence (PRE-EXISTING spark-spawner posture; folds into netcode/save-determinism backlog). (d) hunter disconnect-fade (PDR locks immediate despawn; optional polish). (e) Fork E potato fuse from-SPAWN is one-line-flippable to from-placement (makePotato + applyPlacePotato) if playtest prefers Council's. === CONFIRM AT NEXT BOOT (Rule 22) === P3 (76af09a) + P4 (309024c) post-push CI E2E were in-flight at handoff (local Playwright 27/27 green); confirm both green. === PRE-EXISTING BACKLOG (prioritized for next session; NOT started S72 -- ORANGE budget + each is multi-session) === 1. STABLE LOBBY SEATS (non-compacting) -- likely the most tractable + highest user-value next; bounded scope (lobby/seating). 2. CONTROL-MESSAGE SENDER-AUTH -- security hardening; bounded, raises the v1 threat model past friends-only. 3. EYES fog fuzzy-edge + CVD shape-icons -- render/accessibility feature; self-contained. 4. LIVE-PLAY NETCODE INFRA (host-migration / reconnect / 6p delta) -- LARGEST (genuinely multi-session); also the home for the spawner-rng-serialization + the no-bump cross-version work above. Recommend: tackle #1 or #3 as a single-priority Standard PDR next session; #4 needs its own multi-session plan. === OTHER (pre-existing) === main.ts Pixi/DOM shell hypertrophy (de-hypertrophy candidate).

Grok 1 call this session (~$0.05, premium reasoning).
